children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The National Coordinator shall support the development and routine updating of qualified electronic health record technology (as defined in ) consistent with subsections (b) and (c) and make available such qualified electronic health record technology unless the Secretary determines through an assessment that the needs and demands of providers are being substantially and adequately met through the marketplace.section 300jj of this title

children.heading: Certification
children.label: b Certification
children.text: In making such electronic health record technology publicly available, the National Coordinator shall ensure that the qualified electronic health record technology described in subsection (a) is certified under the program developed under to be in compliance with applicable standards adopted under section 300jj–12(a)(2) of this title.section 300jj–11(c)(3) of this title1So in original. No par. (2) of section 300jj–12(a) has been enacted.1

children.heading: Authorization to charge a nominal fee
children.label: c Authorization to charge a nominal fee
children.text: The National Coordinator may impose a nominal fee for the adoption by a health care provider of the health information technology system developed or approved under subsection (a) and (b). Such fee shall take into account the financial circumstances of smaller providers, low income providers, and providers located in rural or other medically underserved areas.2So in original. Probably should be “subsections”.2

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: d Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to require that a private or government entity adopt or use the technology provided under this section.
